content: Just as language learners solidify new vocabulary and correct usage through repeated explicit reflection (as taught in high school English literacy curricula that introduce 'reflexion' as a core learning practice), large language models can improve their factual accuracy and reasoning consistency by integrating graded, vocabulary-like spaced repetition of self-reflection outputs into their dynamic memory systems. This turns episodic self-reflection on incorrect outputs into durable, retrievable cognitive habits that reduce repeated reasoning errors.
